summary: Vitis coignetiae, commonly called crimson glory vine, is a species of grapevine belonging to the family Vitaceae. Is native to the temperate climes of Asia and can be found in the Russian Far East ( Sakhalin ), Korea, and Japan ( Hokkaido, Honshu, Shikoku ). It was described botanically in 1883. It is called meoru ( 머루 ) in Korean and yama-budo ( ヤマブドウ ) in Japanese.
